summary: Isaac Sim 4.5.0 utilizes the 'isaacsim.core.api.sensors.Camera' class to retrieve camera intrinsics via 'get_intrinsics_matrix()'. The intrinsic matrix K follows the pinhole model where focal lengths are calculated as fx = f * Width / sensor_width and fy = f * Height / sensor_height. Verification is performed by comparing 2D projected coordinates 'u = K * [R|t] * X' against ground truth pixel values. Standard error metrics include Mean Reprojection Error (MRE) and RMSE to quantify the spatial deviation epsilon between simulated and mathematical projections.
code_snippets: from isaacsim.core.api.sensors import Camera; camera = Camera(prim_path='/World/Camera'); K = camera.get_intrinsics_matrix()
P_pixel = K @ (R @ P_world + t); u = P_pixel[0]/P_pixel[2]; v = P_pixel[1]/P_pixel[2]
